content_body:
Father's Day in Blue Ridge, GA

Father's Day in the Blue Ridge Mountains are one of the best gifts for dad this season. It's a package deal: an experience, a memory, and an ode to fatherhood by building those family relationships with time well spent. You can actually skip on the greeting card, he only shakes it for money anyway. Blue Ridge has a ton to offer, whether it's pushing the body's limits by bike riding, testing the limits with patience with fishing - like spinning rods or casting rods - or total leisure on the golf course. You can always buy dad a gift, something simple he'll likely use only when you're around - OAR has some pretty cool items for local souvenirs just in case you forgot - but why not make this a Father's Day weekend he'll never forget?

Fishing Rods at Noontootla Creek

For a special outdoor trip to Blue Ridge, a stay at Cherokee Sunrise on Noontootla Creek is the ideal for a remote fishing trip for the family. It is nestled on 30 acres with a well-stocked pond cost to the farmhouse - with a 20-odd-year-old Koi fish named Charlie - as well as the Noontootla Creek frontage where you can fish without a Guide for about 250ft inward from the bridge. Otherwise, book an incredible adventure with Blue Ridge's finest, Joe DiPietro with North Georgia Trout Fishing; large bows and browns, rainbow trout, and all the equipment for fly fishing is provided! Father's Day things to do bring out the mountaineer in all the men when you join us for what could be the first Father's Day in Blue Ridge, GA, but certainly not the last.

Spending quiet time to bond, to share in your child's dreams, concerns and questions while enjoying an activity which allows for solitude and focus that will be a bonding experience to last a lifetime. Unforgettable moments, successes as well as the "one" that got away moments and memories are yours for the making. The common goal of simply catching fish allows the dynamic duo of parent and child to be on an even field, yet the mature qualities of patience, wisdom and compassion that only a father can provide comes into play. There are many redeeming features about Blue Ridge, but fathers day in the north Georgia mountains have the capacity to be the next best memory, more profound than dad's most overly quoted favorite movie.

Father's Day Vacation: Fishing Poles, Barbeques, & Family Fun

If the whole family is coming to Blue Ridge, GA we have the perfect luxury cabin rental for you along the big waters of the Toccoa River and the Cherokee Fish Trap - ideal for catching fish! This fish trap was installed by indigenous tribesmen by piling rocks and boulders across the river in a distinctive V-shape by funneling the fish from the wide mouth into the tip of the V. Check out Fallen Timber Lodge, A Riverview Lodge, or even The River's Edge for a great family vacation that you will remember forever. 

Outfitted with a community area closer towards the river with campfires and picnic tables alike, your fathers day cabin beats going out to eat by building memories, showing thanks, and sharing family stories. A road trip full of family things to do, Blue Ridge never disappoints. 

Another benefit to these large luxe cabins and their riverfront placement is that they have the capacity to be the launch - exit - for tubing, fishing, kayaking, and anything else water related. Skip the water park, and express gratitude this year by enjoying all that nature has to offer: tube the Toccoa River with Dad, family friendly time, spent well.
title: Extra Special Things to Do with Dad: Father's Day 2022
